jonnify·bcs /bcs/cache/coherence-by-mint-time/the-job-lane
Transcript The crash The price References

B4.2 · dive 3 — the job lane

The lane that survives: at-least-once delivery, exactly-once effect.

The same twenty-nine bytes enqueued on EchoMQ's fair lanes, applied by a consumer running Table.coherence_handler/1. The drill is the bus's own crash choreography from Part III, now carrying coherence — the first consumer died holding the job, the reaper returned it, the healer applied it — and the record closes on the chapter's one-row summary: broadcast median 72 us fire-and-forget, job lane median 148 us at-least-once -- the guarantee costs 2.1 times the latency.

Source: content/bcs4.2.md, quoting bcs_rung_4_2_check.out. This dive reads the job-lane derive, the staged kill, F5, the price derive, and F6 — the derived expectation: attempts 2 and exactly one effective drop.

§1

The transcript

bcs_rung_4_2_check.out · verbatim · this dive reads the derives, the kill, F5, and F6

The mid-record [error] GenServer … terminating block is the staged consumer kill of the F5 drill — part of the frozen record, not noise around it. The derive line before it states the expectation the gate then asserts: attempts 2 and exactly one effective drop.

§2

The crash choreography

content/bcs4.2.md · What · Decisions

Every part of the drill is Part III machinery doing its committed work — the lease that strands the job when the consumer dies, the reaper that returns it, the second claim that hands token 2. Redelivery is harmless because application is a comparison; the provenance machinery of 3.5 is not even needed here — the version is the provenance. And the completed job left no row to browse: completion deletes on this bus, so the lane's last word is the cache state itself — :qc dropped its stale row and now serves px=107.00 from the shared L2.

The job-lane consumer lives in the application's tree. The table wires its own broadcast subscription — cheap, self-contained, supervision-as-resubscription — but an EchoMQ consumer is a real supervised worker with a lease and a lane, and hiding one inside a cache table would falsify the supervision tree. coherence_handler/1 makes the wiring one line; the tree stays true:

{:ok, _} =
  EchoMQ.Consumer.start_link(
    queue: Coherence.queue("quotes"),
    connector: [port: 6390],
    handler: Table.coherence_handler(:quotes)
  )

The lease, the reaper, and the fencing token under this drill are the machine B3.3 · The State Machine in Lua gated; the enqueue and the idempotent row are B3.2 · Jobs Are Entities's. The job lane adds nothing to either — it carries the twenty-nine bytes across machinery that already proved itself.

§3

The price, on one row

content/bcs4.2.md · What · When · Boundaries

The derive line prices the shape before the stopwatch runs: the broadcast lane is one wire hop; the job lane pays three to five hops — enqueue, wake, claim, complete — so a parked consumer should land its median between 80 us and 2 ms, the same order as the bus's committed 0.3 ms end-to-end median, carrying the guarantee the push cannot. The measurement: broadcast median 72 us fire-and-forget, job lane median 148 us at-least-once -- the guarantee costs 2.1 times the latency.

Both lanes are sub-millisecond on this wire; the real difference is gates F4 and F5, not the microseconds — the lane a surface declares follows from what a stale read costs, and the declaration in the directory records the choice. Declare :job when at-least-once is a requirement — and note what the record shows: the guarantee's latency price on this wire is 76 microseconds, so the job lane is not the slow lane, it is the accountable one. Risk and limits surfaces ride this lane because a stale ceiling is money; quote boards ride broadcast — or nothing — because a TTL was already enough.

Boundaries, stated honestly: the job lane inherits the bus's contract — at-least-once with redelivery, volatile by D-2 — a bus restart loses queued coherence jobs along with everything else, and the TTL is again the floor under the loss. The latencies are this container's: one core, loopback, both lanes far below any network's floor — the ratio and the ordering travel, the microseconds do not. And the applier is the owner — for now: pushes and jobs apply inline in the table's process, which is correct and measured fast; B4.3 · The Single Writer and the Ring moves application onto the ring for batching and backpressure without touching the semantics gated here.

§4

References

sources · related

Sources

  • Valkey — Pub/Subthe contract the broadcast side of the priced pair inherits
  • Valkey — Client-side cachingthe comparison set's deletion-shaped coherence: no version, no order, no lane named per surface
  • Lamport — Time, Clocks, and the Ordering of Eventswhy reapplication is harmless: the order is total and lives in the name
